search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng77 cataracts adults referral visual function postoperative assessment
site:college-optometrists.org cataract referral visual function guidance
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Surgical technique and lens selection were outside this documentation workflow.
Medicine names and doses were outside scope.
Surgery and referral remain preference-sensitive clinician decisions.
population_applicability: Partial: NICE applies to adults with cataracts before and after surgery; the legacy workflow does not distinguish monitoring from postoperative review and has no age filter.
setting_applicability: Exact for selected cataract information, vision or quality-of-life impact, surgery discussion, postoperative information, and review fields; red-eye warnings are supported by DHA telehealth guidance.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE cataract follow-up guideline was identified. DHA red-eye warnings are direct for Dubai telehealth; NICE cataract pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: NICE NG77, last reviewed 2025-05-20 with a selective future update planned, and current DHA Issue 2 conjunctivitis guidance were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NICE NG77 remains current and no newer DHA conjunctivitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports cataract identity, reduced vision, functional or quality-of-life impact, laterality discussion, and clinician-documented cataract or surgery discussion.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented visual change, activity or driving discussion, spectacles, questions, next appointment, visual-function review, and follow-up without medicine details.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports pain, red eye, sudden or reduced vision, and photophobia warning prompts in a broad red-eye differential.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports visual review and assessed red-eye history without inferring a cataract complication.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented earlier review when pain, visual change, or red-eye concerns worsen without automatic referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Blurred or cloudy vision wording, glare, halos, night-driving difficulty, lens opacity, red reflex, eye pressure, discharge, fixed three-to-six-month review, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not diagnose cataract, infer complication or urgency, recommend surgery or referral, or add medicine or driving advice unless clinician-documented.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
